questions.question: What service runs on TCP port 22 by default?
answers: SSH (Secure Shell) — provides encrypted remote login and file transfer
Telnet — provides unencrypted remote terminal access over TCP
RDP — Microsoft's protocol for graphical remote access to Windows
RSH — a legacy Unix remote shell with no encryption support
questions.correct: SSH (Secure Shell) — provides encrypted remote login and file transfer
questions.explanation: Port 22 is SSH. It replaced Telnet and RSH by encrypting authentication and session data. SFTP and SCP also run over port 22 as SSH subsystems.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 23?
answers: Telnet — unencrypted remote terminal protocol; transmits credentials in cleartext
SSH — encrypted remote terminal protocol with strong authentication
RSH — legacy remote shell used on early Unix network systems
RDP — Microsoft remote desktop protocol for Windows graphical sessions
questions.correct: Telnet — unencrypted remote terminal protocol; transmits credentials in cleartext
questions.explanation: Port 23 is Telnet. It sends all data including usernames and passwords in plaintext. SSH on port 22 is its encrypted replacement and should be used instead.

questions.question: What service runs on TCP port 80?
answers: HTTP — Hypertext Transfer Protocol for unencrypted web traffic
HTTPS — HTTP over TLS providing encrypted web communication
FTP — File Transfer Protocol for uploading and downloading files
SMTP — Simple Mail Transfer Protocol for sending email messages
questions.correct: HTTP — Hypertext Transfer Protocol for unencrypted web traffic
questions.explanation: Port 80 is HTTP. Traffic is unencrypted and vulnerable to interception. Organizations should redirect HTTP to HTTPS (port 443) to enforce encryption across all web traffic.

questions.question: What service runs on TCP port 443?
answers: HTTPS — HTTP over TLS/SSL providing encrypted web communication
HTTP — standard unencrypted Hypertext Transfer Protocol
FTPS — FTP over SSL providing encrypted file transfers
SSH — Secure Shell for encrypted remote system administration
questions.correct: HTTPS — HTTP over TLS/SSL providing encrypted web communication
questions.explanation: Port 443 is HTTPS. It uses TLS to encrypt traffic between browsers and servers, protecting data from eavesdropping and tampering. All public-facing web services should use 443.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 25?
answers: SMTP — Simple Mail Transfer Protocol for server-to-server mail relay
POP3 — Post Office Protocol for downloading email from mail servers
IMAP — Internet Message Access Protocol for remote mailbox access
SMTPS — SMTP over TLS for encrypted mail server communication
questions.correct: SMTP — Simple Mail Transfer Protocol for server-to-server mail relay
questions.explanation: Port 25 is SMTP relay, used between mail servers. It should be blocked for outbound connections from end-user workstations to prevent spam. Clients should use port 587 for authenticated submission.

questions.question: What port should email clients use to submit outbound mail to a mail server?
answers: TCP port 587 — SMTP submission port requiring client authentication
TCP port 25 — SMTP relay port intended for server-to-server traffic
TCP port 465 — SMTPS legacy port using implicit SSL/TLS encryption
TCP port 110 — POP3 port for downloading mail from a server
questions.correct: TCP port 587 — SMTP submission port requiring client authentication
questions.explanation: Port 587 is the SMTP submission port. Unlike port 25 (server relay), port 587 requires authentication, making it the correct port for mail clients sending outbound messages.

questions.question: What is TCP port 465 used for?
answers: SMTPS — SMTP over implicit SSL/TLS for encrypted mail submission
SMTP submission — authenticated outbound mail from email clients
IMAPS — IMAP over TLS for encrypted retrieval of mail messages
POP3S — POP3 over TLS for encrypted download of mail messages
questions.correct: SMTPS — SMTP over implicit SSL/TLS for encrypted mail submission
questions.explanation: Port 465 is SMTPS, using implicit SSL. While port 587 with STARTTLS is now preferred, many servers still support 465 for legacy compatibility.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 110?
answers: POP3 — Post Office Protocol v3 for downloading email (unencrypted)
IMAP — Internet Message Access Protocol for remote mailbox management
SMTP — Simple Mail Transfer Protocol for sending and relaying email
POP3S — POP3 over TLS providing encrypted email download sessions
questions.correct: POP3 — Post Office Protocol v3 for downloading email (unencrypted)
questions.explanation: Port 110 is POP3. It downloads mail from the server to the client and typically deletes server copies. Port 995 (POP3S) is the encrypted version.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 143?
answers: IMAP — Internet Message Access Protocol for remote mailbox management
POP3 — Post Office Protocol for downloading email to local clients
SMTP — Simple Mail Transfer Protocol for sending outbound email
IMAPS — IMAP over TLS for encrypted remote mailbox access
questions.correct: IMAP — Internet Message Access Protocol for remote mailbox management
questions.explanation: Port 143 is IMAP. Unlike POP3, IMAP keeps mail on the server and syncs across multiple clients. Port 993 (IMAPS) is the TLS-encrypted version.

questions.question: What port does IMAPS (IMAP over TLS) use?
answers: TCP port 993 — IMAP with TLS encryption for secure mailbox access
TCP port 995 — POP3 with TLS encryption for secure mail download
TCP port 465 — SMTP with SSL encryption for secure mail submission
TCP port 636 — LDAP with TLS encryption for secure directory access
questions.correct: TCP port 993 — IMAP with TLS encryption for secure mailbox access
questions.explanation: Port 993 is IMAPS. It encrypts the entire IMAP session using TLS. Organizations should disable plaintext IMAP on port 143 and enforce IMAPS.

questions.question: What port does POP3S (POP3 over TLS) use?
answers: TCP port 995 — POP3 with TLS encryption for secure mail download
TCP port 993 — IMAP with TLS encryption for secure mailbox access
TCP port 465 — SMTP with SSL encryption for secure mail submission
TCP port 587 — SMTP submission with authentication for outbound mail
questions.correct: TCP port 995 — POP3 with TLS encryption for secure mail download
questions.explanation: Port 995 is POP3S. It uses TLS to protect the session. Plaintext POP3 on port 110 should be disabled and POP3S or IMAPS enforced.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 21?
answers: FTP control — command channel for FTP session setup and control
FTP data — data transfer channel for file content and directory listings
SSH — encrypted remote shell and file transfer over a single port
TFTP — Trivial File Transfer Protocol using UDP for simple transfers
questions.correct: FTP control — command channel for FTP session setup and control
questions.explanation: Port 21 is the FTP control channel where commands are sent. Port 20 carries file data in active mode FTP. FTP sends credentials in plaintext and should be replaced with SFTP or FTPS.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 20?
answers: FTP data — transfers file content and directory listings in active mode
FTP control — command channel for FTP authentication and control
SSH — encrypted remote shell that also handles file transfers via SFTP
TFTP — Trivial File Transfer Protocol for diskless client booting
questions.correct: FTP data — transfers file content and directory listings in active mode
questions.explanation: Port 20 is the FTP active-mode data channel. The server initiates from port 20 to deliver file data. In passive mode, a random ephemeral port is negotiated instead.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 69?
answers: TFTP — Trivial File Transfer Protocol for booting and simple transfers
FTP — File Transfer Protocol for full-featured client-server file sharing
SFTP — SSH File Transfer Protocol for encrypted file management
NFS — Network File System for mounting remote file systems
questions.correct: TFTP — Trivial File Transfer Protocol for booting and simple transfers
questions.explanation: Port 69 UDP is TFTP. It has no authentication and no encryption, making it unsuitable for sensitive data. It is commonly used for PXE boot and transferring network device firmware/configs.

questions.question: What port does FTPS (FTP over SSL) use for the control channel?
answers: TCP port 990 — FTPS control channel using implicit TLS encryption
TCP port 989 — FTPS data channel for encrypted file transfers
TCP port 21 — standard FTP control channel (unencrypted)
TCP port 22 — SSH port used by SFTP for encrypted transfers
questions.correct: TCP port 990 — FTPS control channel using implicit TLS encryption
questions.explanation: Port 990 is FTPS control (implicit TLS). Port 989 carries FTPS data. SFTP is a completely different protocol over port 22 and is generally preferred over FTPS.

questions.question: What port does FTPS use for data transfers?
answers: TCP port 989 — FTPS data channel with implicit TLS encryption
TCP port 990 — FTPS control channel for session setup and commands
TCP port 20 — unencrypted FTP active-mode data channel
TCP port 21 — unencrypted FTP control channel for commands
questions.correct: TCP port 989 — FTPS data channel with implicit TLS encryption
questions.explanation: Port 989 is the FTPS data channel. FTPS uses 990 for control and 989 for data, both over TLS. This is distinct from FTP (20/21) and SFTP (22).

questions.question: What service uses UDP or TCP port 53?
answers: DNS — Domain Name System for resolving hostnames to IP addresses
DHCP — Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol for IP address assignment
NTP — Network Time Protocol for synchronizing clocks across systems
SNMP — Simple Network Management Protocol for device monitoring
questions.correct: DNS — Domain Name System for resolving hostnames to IP addresses
questions.explanation: Port 53 is DNS. UDP is used for most queries; TCP is used for zone transfers and large responses. DNS is a common data exfiltration channel and should be monitored closely.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 67?
answers: DHCP server — receives discovery and request messages from clients
DHCP client — receives address assignment offers from DHCP servers
DNS server — receives name resolution queries from client systems
TFTP server — receives file transfer requests from network clients
questions.correct: DHCP server — receives discovery and request messages from clients
questions.explanation: Port 67 UDP is the DHCP server port. Clients broadcast to this port. Rogue DHCP servers exploiting port 67 can redirect traffic by assigning malicious gateway and DNS settings.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 68?
answers: DHCP client — receives IP address offers from DHCP servers
DHCP server — listens for discovery and request messages from clients
DNS client — sends hostname resolution queries to DNS servers
TFTP client — receives file data transferred from a TFTP server
questions.correct: DHCP client — receives IP address offers from DHCP servers
questions.explanation: Port 68 UDP is the DHCP client port. The server sends offers and ACKs to this port. A rogue DHCP server can intercept DHCP to assign attacker-controlled gateway and DNS addresses.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 123?
answers: NTP — Network Time Protocol for clock synchronization across systems
SNMP — Simple Network Management Protocol for device monitoring
Syslog — remote logging protocol for forwarding system event messages
TFTP — Trivial File Transfer Protocol for simple file transfers
questions.correct: NTP — Network Time Protocol for clock synchronization across systems
questions.explanation: Port 123 UDP is NTP. Accurate time is critical for security — Kerberos fails if clocks differ by more than 5 minutes, and log correlation requires synchronized timestamps across systems.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 161?
answers: SNMP — agent port for polling and reading network device information
SNMP trap — port for unsolicited alerts sent from agents to managers
Syslog — port for collecting remote system log messages
NTP — port for network time synchronization requests
questions.correct: SNMP — agent port for polling and reading network device information
questions.explanation: Port 161 UDP is SNMP. Managers poll agents on 161 to read MIB data. SNMPv1/v2c use cleartext community strings; SNMPv3 adds authentication and encryption and should be used instead.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 162?
answers: SNMP trap — unsolicited alerts sent from agents to network managers
SNMP polling — manager-initiated queries to read device MIB data
Syslog — remote system log message forwarding to a central server
NTP — time synchronization response messages from time servers
questions.correct: SNMP trap — unsolicited alerts sent from agents to network managers
questions.explanation: Port 162 UDP is SNMP traps. Unlike normal SNMP (161) where the manager polls the agent, traps are agent-initiated to notify the manager of events like interface failures or threshold alerts.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 514?
answers: Syslog — protocol for forwarding system log messages to a remote server
SNMP — protocol for monitoring and managing network device status
TFTP — protocol for simple file transfers without authentication
NTP — protocol for synchronizing clocks across networked systems
questions.correct: Syslog — protocol for forwarding system log messages to a remote server
questions.explanation: Port 514 UDP is Syslog. Devices forward log events to a central syslog server. Basic Syslog has no authentication or integrity protection; syslog-ng and rsyslog support TLS-encrypted transport.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 389?
answers: LDAP — Lightweight Directory Access Protocol for directory queries (unencrypted)
LDAPS — LDAP over TLS for encrypted directory access
Kerberos — network authentication protocol using tickets
RADIUS — remote authentication and authorization service
questions.correct: LDAP — Lightweight Directory Access Protocol for directory queries (unencrypted)
questions.explanation: Port 389 is LDAP. It provides access to directory services like Active Directory in plaintext. Port 636 (LDAPS) or LDAP with STARTTLS should be used to encrypt directory credentials and queries.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 636?
answers: LDAPS — LDAP over TLS providing encrypted directory access
LDAP — Lightweight Directory Access Protocol for unencrypted queries
Kerberos — ticket-based network authentication protocol
TACACS+ — terminal access control for device administration
questions.correct: LDAPS — LDAP over TLS providing encrypted directory access
questions.explanation: Port 636 is LDAPS (LDAP Secure). It wraps LDAP in TLS to protect directory queries and credentials. Disabling port 389 and enforcing LDAPS is a standard hardening practice.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 88?
answers: Kerberos — ticket-based authentication protocol used in Active Directory
LDAP — Lightweight Directory Access Protocol for directory services
RADIUS — Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service
TACACS+ — Terminal Access Controller Access Control System Plus
questions.correct: Kerberos — ticket-based authentication protocol used in Active Directory
questions.explanation: Port 88 is Kerberos. Active Directory relies on Kerberos for authentication. The Key Distribution Center (KDC) issues tickets so passwords are never sent directly across the network.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 3268?
answers: LDAP Global Catalog — allows searching all objects in an AD forest
LDAPS Global Catalog — encrypted AD forest-wide directory searches
LDAP — standard directory access within a single AD domain
Kerberos — ticket-based authentication for Active Directory domains
questions.correct: LDAP Global Catalog — allows searching all objects in an AD forest
questions.explanation: Port 3268 is the LDAP Global Catalog. It enables clients to query any object across all domains in an AD forest. Port 3269 is the encrypted (LDAPS) version of the Global Catalog.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 3389?
answers: RDP — Remote Desktop Protocol for graphical remote access to Windows
VNC — Virtual Network Computing for remote desktop across platforms
SSH — Secure Shell for encrypted command-line remote administration
Telnet — unencrypted remote terminal protocol for legacy systems
questions.correct: RDP — Remote Desktop Protocol for graphical remote access to Windows
questions.explanation: Port 3389 is RDP. It is a frequent attack target for brute force and credential stuffing. Best practice is to keep RDP behind a VPN or restrict it to a jump host — never expose it directly to the internet.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 5900?
answers: VNC — Virtual Network Computing for cross-platform remote desktop access
RDP — Remote Desktop Protocol for Windows remote desktop sessions
SSH — Secure Shell for encrypted remote command-line administration
X11 — X Window System for forwarding graphical apps over a network
questions.correct: VNC — Virtual Network Computing for cross-platform remote desktop access
questions.explanation: Port 5900 is VNC. Older VNC implementations have weak authentication. VNC should always be tunneled through SSH or accessed only over a VPN to prevent unauthorized access.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 445?
answers: SMB — Server Message Block for Windows file and printer sharing
NetBIOS Session Service — legacy Windows session service over TCP
LDAP — Lightweight Directory Access Protocol for Active Directory
RPC — Remote Procedure Call for Windows service communication
questions.correct: SMB — Server Message Block for Windows file and printer sharing
questions.explanation: Port 445 is SMB. The EternalBlue exploit (MS17-010), which powered WannaCry and NotPetya, targeted this port. Port 445 should be blocked at perimeter firewalls and patched on all internal hosts.

questions.question: What service uses TCP/UDP port 137?
answers: NetBIOS Name Service — resolves NetBIOS names to IP addresses
NetBIOS Datagram Service — connectionless NetBIOS data transfers
NetBIOS Session Service — connection-oriented NetBIOS sessions
SMB — Server Message Block for Windows file and print sharing
questions.correct: NetBIOS Name Service — resolves NetBIOS names to IP addresses
questions.explanation: Port 137 is NetBIOS Name Service (NBNS). It resolves NetBIOS names, similar to DNS. It should be blocked at perimeter firewalls as it exposes internal hostnames to attackers.

questions.question: What service uses TCP/UDP port 139?
answers: NetBIOS Session Service — connection-oriented NetBIOS sessions
NetBIOS Name Service — resolves NetBIOS names to IP addresses
NetBIOS Datagram Service — connectionless NetBIOS communications
SMB — modern Server Message Block direct hosting on Windows
questions.correct: NetBIOS Session Service — connection-oriented NetBIOS sessions
questions.explanation: Port 139 is NetBIOS Session Service, used for SMB over NetBIOS on legacy Windows. Modern Windows uses port 445 directly. Both 139 and 445 should be blocked from internet exposure.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 135?
answers: MS RPC — Microsoft Remote Procedure Call endpoint mapper
SMB — Server Message Block for Windows file and printer sharing
NetBIOS — legacy Windows network name and session services
WinRM — Windows Remote Management for PowerShell remoting
questions.correct: MS RPC — Microsoft Remote Procedure Call endpoint mapper
questions.explanation: Port 135 is the MS RPC endpoint mapper. Many Windows services use RPC and register with the endpoint mapper on port 135. It should be blocked at perimeter firewalls — it is not needed externally.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 179?
answers: BGP — Border Gateway Protocol for inter-autonomous system routing
OSPF — Open Shortest Path First for intra-domain link-state routing
RIP — Routing Information Protocol for simple distance-vector routing
EIGRP — Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol by Cisco
questions.correct: BGP — Border Gateway Protocol for inter-autonomous system routing
questions.explanation: Port 179 is BGP, the routing protocol of the internet. BGP hijacking can redirect global traffic. BGP sessions should be protected with TCP MD5 or TCP-AO authentication.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 500?
answers: IKE/ISAKMP — IPSec key exchange for establishing VPN tunnel security associations
L2TP — Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol for VPN encapsulation
PPTP — Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol for legacy VPN connections
GRE — Generic Routing Encapsulation for IP tunneling
questions.correct: IKE/ISAKMP — IPSec key exchange for establishing VPN tunnel security associations
questions.explanation: Port 500 UDP is IKE (Internet Key Exchange), used to negotiate IPSec security associations. IPSec also uses UDP 4500 for NAT Traversal to work through NAT devices.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 1701?
answers: L2TP — Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol, typically paired with IPSec
PPTP — Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol for legacy VPN tunnels
IKE — Internet Key Exchange for IPSec tunnel negotiation
OpenVPN — open-source VPN using TLS for key exchange and encryption
questions.correct: L2TP — Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol, typically paired with IPSec
questions.explanation: Port 1701 UDP is L2TP. L2TP provides no encryption on its own and is typically combined with IPSec (L2TP/IPSec) for security. It is supported natively by Windows VPN clients.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 1723?
answers: PPTP — Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol for legacy VPN connections
L2TP — Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol for VPN tunneling paired with IPSec
IKE — Internet Key Exchange for IPSec security association setup
SSL VPN — VPN over HTTPS using TLS for encrypted remote access
questions.correct: PPTP — Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol for legacy VPN connections
questions.explanation: Port 1723 is PPTP. PPTP is considered insecure due to weaknesses in MS-CHAPv2 authentication and MPPE encryption. L2TP/IPSec, IKEv2, or OpenVPN should be used instead.

questions.question: What port does OpenVPN use by default?
answers: UDP port 1194 — OpenVPN default for fast, reliable VPN tunneling
TCP port 443 — alternative for OpenVPN to blend with HTTPS traffic
UDP port 500 — IKE port used for IPSec key exchange negotiations
UDP port 1701 — L2TP port for Layer 2 VPN encapsulation
questions.correct: UDP port 1194 — OpenVPN default for fast, reliable VPN tunneling
questions.explanation: OpenVPN defaults to UDP 1194. It can also run on TCP 443 to bypass firewalls blocking VPN traffic. OpenVPN uses TLS for authentication and key exchange with the OpenSSL library.

questions.question: Which port pair does IPSec use when NAT Traversal (NAT-T) is required?
answers: UDP 500 (IKE) and UDP 4500 (NAT-T) for IPSec through NAT devices
UDP 500 (IKE) and TCP 1723 (PPTP) for combined VPN tunneling
UDP 1701 (L2TP) and UDP 500 (IKE) for L2TP/IPSec connections
TCP 443 (HTTPS) and UDP 500 (IKE) for SSL VPN with IPSec fallback
questions.correct: UDP 500 (IKE) and UDP 4500 (NAT-T) for IPSec through NAT devices
questions.explanation: IPSec uses UDP 500 for IKE and UDP 4500 for NAT Traversal. NAT-T encapsulates ESP packets in UDP so they can traverse NAT devices, which would otherwise break IPSec integrity checks.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 3306?
answers: MySQL / MariaDB — open-source relational database server
Microsoft SQL Server — Microsoft's enterprise relational database
Oracle Database — Oracle's enterprise relational database listener
PostgreSQL — open-source object-relational database system
questions.correct: MySQL / MariaDB — open-source relational database server
questions.explanation: Port 3306 is MySQL (and its fork MariaDB). Database ports should never be exposed to the internet. Firewall rules should restrict access to application servers only.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 1433?
answers: Microsoft SQL Server — Microsoft's enterprise relational database engine
MySQL — open-source relational database management system
Oracle Database — Oracle's enterprise relational database listener
PostgreSQL — open-source relational database management system
questions.correct: Microsoft SQL Server — Microsoft's enterprise relational database engine
questions.explanation: Port 1433 is Microsoft SQL Server. A SQL Server instance exposed to the internet can be attacked via brute force or SQL injection. This port should be firewalled and never internet-facing.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 1521?
answers: Oracle Database — Oracle's default listener port for database connections
Microsoft SQL Server — Microsoft's enterprise database engine port
MySQL — open-source database widely used in web applications
IBM DB2 — IBM's enterprise relational database management system
questions.correct: Oracle Database — Oracle's default listener port for database connections
questions.explanation: Port 1521 is Oracle Database's default listener. Like all database ports, it must be firewalled from external access and restricted to authorized application servers by network ACL.

questions.question: What service uses TCP/UDP port 5060?
answers: SIP — Session Initiation Protocol for VoIP call signaling (unencrypted)
SIP over TLS — encrypted SIP signaling for secure VoIP sessions
H.323 — legacy VoIP signaling protocol for video conferencing
RTP — Real-time Transport Protocol for voice and video media streams
questions.correct: SIP — Session Initiation Protocol for VoIP call signaling (unencrypted)
questions.explanation: Port 5060 is SIP. It sets up, manages, and terminates VoIP calls in cleartext. Port 5061 uses TLS. Exposed SIP ports are targets for eavesdropping, toll fraud, and SIP flooding attacks.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 5061?
answers: SIP over TLS — encrypted SIP signaling for secure VoIP sessions
SIP — unencrypted Session Initiation Protocol for VoIP signaling
H.323 — legacy video conferencing and VoIP signaling protocol
MGCP — Media Gateway Control Protocol for VoIP gateways
questions.correct: SIP over TLS — encrypted SIP signaling for secure VoIP sessions
questions.explanation: Port 5061 is SIPS (SIP Secure). It encrypts SIP signaling with TLS. Note that SRTP must also be used to encrypt the media stream (RTP) for full end-to-end VoIP security.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 1720?
answers: H.323 — legacy VoIP and video conferencing signaling protocol
SIP — Session Initiation Protocol for modern VoIP communications
RTP — Real-time Transport Protocol for audio and video media streams
MGCP — Media Gateway Control Protocol for telephony gateways
questions.correct: H.323 — legacy VoIP and video conferencing signaling protocol
questions.explanation: Port 1720 is H.323, an older VoIP and video conferencing protocol. It has largely been replaced by SIP. H.323 uses multiple dynamic ports for media channels, complicating firewall configuration.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 1812?
answers: RADIUS authentication — remote authentication and authorization service
RADIUS accounting — tracks session data for billing and auditing
TACACS+ — terminal access controller access control system
Kerberos — ticket-based authentication for network services
questions.correct: RADIUS authentication — remote authentication and authorization service
questions.explanation: Port 1812 UDP is RADIUS authentication. RADIUS centralizes authentication for Wi-Fi, VPN, and switch access. It supports EAP for 802.1X. Port 1813 is used for RADIUS accounting.

questions.question: What service uses UDP port 1813?
answers: RADIUS accounting — logs session start, stop, and usage statistics
RADIUS authentication — verifies credentials for network access
TACACS+ — Cisco's alternative to RADIUS for device administration
Diameter — next-generation AAA protocol replacing RADIUS in LTE
questions.correct: RADIUS accounting — logs session start, stop, and usage statistics
questions.explanation: Port 1813 UDP is RADIUS accounting. It tracks when users log in and out and how much data they use — useful for billing, compliance, and forensic analysis of network access events.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 49?
answers: TACACS+ — Terminal Access Controller Access Control System Plus
RADIUS — Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service
Kerberos — MIT-developed ticket-based authentication protocol
LDAP — Lightweight Directory Access Protocol for directory queries
questions.correct: TACACS+ — Terminal Access Controller Access Control System Plus
questions.explanation: Port 49 is TACACS+. Unlike RADIUS which combines authentication and authorization, TACACS+ separates them and encrypts the entire packet body. It is preferred for managing router and switch access.

questions.question: What service uses TCP/UDP port 2049?
answers: NFS — Network File System for mounting remote file systems
SMB — Server Message Block for Windows file and printer sharing
FTP — File Transfer Protocol for client-server file transfers
iSCSI — Internet Small Computer System Interface for block storage
questions.correct: NFS — Network File System for mounting remote file systems
questions.explanation: Port 2049 is NFS. Used primarily in Unix/Linux environments to mount remote file systems. Older NFS versions lack strong authentication; NFSv4 with Kerberos (GSSAPI) provides much stronger security.

questions.question: What service uses TCP port 119?
answers: NNTP — Network News Transfer Protocol for Usenet newsgroup access
IMAP — Internet Message Access Protocol for email retrieval
POP3 — Post Office Protocol for downloading email from servers
SMTP — Simple Mail Transfer Protocol for sending email messages
questions.correct: NNTP — Network News Transfer Protocol for Usenet newsgroup access
questions.explanation: Port 119 is NNTP (Network News Transfer Protocol) for Usenet. While largely replaced by web forums, it appears on Security+ exam port lists and should be recognized.

questions.question: What service typically runs on TCP port 8080?
answers: HTTP alternate — used by web proxies, app servers, and dev environments
HTTPS alternate — encrypted web traffic on a non-standard port
SMTP alternate — mail relay on a non-standard port for ISP bypass
SSH alternate — remote shell moved to a non-standard port
questions.correct: HTTP alternate — used by web proxies, app servers, and dev environments
questions.explanation: Port 8080 is a common HTTP alternate used by proxies (Squid), app servers (Tomcat), and dev environments. Traffic is unencrypted like port 80. It should not be used for production HTTPS traffic.

questions.question: What service is typically associated with TCP port 8443?
answers: HTTPS alternate — encrypted web traffic on a non-standard port
HTTP alternate — unencrypted web traffic on a non-standard port
SMTP alternate — encrypted mail relay on a non-standard port
SSH alternate — encrypted remote shell on a non-standard port
questions.correct: HTTPS alternate — encrypted web traffic on a non-standard port
questions.explanation: Port 8443 is an alternate HTTPS port used by Tomcat, Jenkins, and other application servers. It carries TLS-encrypted traffic the same as standard port 443.

questions.question: Which port is associated with Metasploit's default Meterpreter listener?
answers: TCP port 4444 — Metasploit's default reverse shell handler port
TCP port 443 — HTTPS port often used for C2 traffic to blend in
TCP port 8080 — HTTP alternate port used by some web shells
TCP port 1337 — informal port associated with hacking culture
questions.correct: TCP port 4444 — Metasploit's default reverse shell handler port
questions.explanation: Port 4444 is Metasploit's default Meterpreter listener. Attackers often switch to port 443 or 80 to evade firewalls. Unexpected traffic to/from port 4444 should trigger a security alert.

questions.question: A firewall rule blocks all outbound TCP port 25 from workstations. What is the primary security benefit?
answers: Prevents workstations from acting as spam relays or botnet mail nodes
Stops users from browsing unencrypted HTTP websites
Blocks unauthorized SSH tunnels from internal workstations
Prevents DNS tunneling used to exfiltrate data externally
questions.correct: Prevents workstations from acting as spam relays or botnet mail nodes
questions.explanation: Blocking outbound TCP 25 from workstations prevents malware from sending spam or C2 traffic directly via SMTP. Legitimate mail clients should use port 587 (submission) through the corporate mail server.

questions.question: An attacker exploits MS17-010 (EternalBlue). Which port was most likely exposed?
answers: TCP port 445 — SMB vulnerability exploited by WannaCry and NotPetya
TCP port 3389 — RDP vulnerability allowing remote code execution
TCP port 139 — NetBIOS Session Service for legacy Windows sharing
TCP port 135 — MS RPC endpoint mapper for Windows services
questions.correct: TCP port 445 — SMB vulnerability exploited by WannaCry and NotPetya
questions.explanation: EternalBlue targets SMBv1 on port 445. It powered both WannaCry and NotPetya ransomware. Port 445 must be blocked at perimeter firewalls and SMBv1 disabled on all internal hosts.

questions.question: An admin discovers inbound connections on port 23 from an external IP. What is the primary concern?
answers: Cleartext credential exposure — Telnet transmits all data including passwords unencrypted
Brute force against SSH — encrypted remote shell targeted by credential stuffing
Web application attack — unencrypted HTTP exploited to steal session tokens
SNMP community string leak — network management data exposed externally
questions.correct: Cleartext credential exposure — Telnet transmits all data including passwords unencrypted
questions.explanation: Port 23 is Telnet. Any external connection here risks credential interception via a man-in-the-middle attack or network capture. Telnet should be disabled entirely and replaced with SSH.

questions.question: A penetration tester finds port 1433 open on an internet-facing server. What is the primary risk?
answers: Direct database access — attacker can brute force or exploit SQL Server remotely
Web application attack — port 1433 exposes an HTTP interface to attackers
RDP brute force — attackers attempt password guessing on remote desktop
DNS hijacking — attackers intercept and modify DNS resolution responses
questions.correct: Direct database access — attacker can brute force or exploit SQL Server remotely
questions.explanation: Port 1433 (MSSQL) exposed to the internet allows direct database attacks including brute force, credential stuffing, and exploitation of unpatched vulnerabilities. Database ports must never be internet-facing.

questions.question: Which port, if open on an internet-facing server, poses the greatest risk due to cleartext credential transmission?
answers: TCP port 23 (Telnet) — transmits all data including passwords unencrypted
TCP port 22 (SSH) — uses strong encryption for all session data
TCP port 443 (HTTPS) — encrypts all traffic using TLS
TCP port 3389 (RDP) — uses TLS to encrypt remote desktop sessions
questions.correct: TCP port 23 (Telnet) — transmits all data including passwords unencrypted
questions.explanation: Port 23 (Telnet) is the highest risk because credentials travel in plaintext. SSH (22), HTTPS (443), and modern RDP (3389) all encrypt session traffic. Telnet must never be exposed to the internet.

questions.question: DNS primarily uses UDP port 53. When is TCP port 53 used instead?
answers: Zone transfers and responses over 512 bytes require TCP port 53
All DNS queries from Windows clients default to TCP port 53
DNSSEC-signed responses always use TCP for reliable delivery
DNS-over-HTTPS uses TCP port 53 for encrypted name resolution
questions.correct: Zone transfers and responses over 512 bytes require TCP port 53
questions.explanation: DNS uses UDP 53 for standard queries. TCP 53 is used for zone transfers (AXFR) and large responses. Zone transfers should be restricted to authorized secondary name servers only.

questions.question: Why is TFTP (port 69) considered insecure for production environments?
answers: TFTP has no authentication and no encryption — any host can request any file
TFTP uses TCP which creates overhead making it slower and less reliable
TFTP requires root privileges to bind privileged ports below 1024
TFTP cleartext credentials can be captured by any network sniffer
questions.correct: TFTP has no authentication and no encryption — any host can request any file
questions.explanation: TFTP (UDP 69) has zero authentication — no username, no password. Any reachable host can request any file. TFTP should be isolated on management VLANs and never exposed externally or to untrusted hosts.

questions.question: Which of the following represents the most secure replacement for FTP on port 21?
answers: SFTP on port 22 — SSH File Transfer Protocol with full encryption and authentication
FTPS on port 990 — FTP over SSL adding TLS encryption to standard FTP
TFTP on port 69 — Trivial FTP for simple transfers without authentication
SCP on port 22 — Secure Copy using SSH for one-way file transfer only
questions.correct: SFTP on port 22 — SSH File Transfer Protocol with full encryption and authentication
questions.explanation: SFTP (port 22) is the most secure FTP replacement. It runs as an SSH subsystem providing strong authentication, full encryption, and a rich feature set. FTPS (990) is also acceptable but requires separate PKI.

questions.question: A security audit flags port 161 as open on a core router with public access. What is the risk?
answers: SNMP information disclosure — MIB data reveals topology, routes, and config details
FTP credential theft — unencrypted login credentials exposed to attackers
Telnet session hijacking — cleartext remote admin sessions intercepted
DNS cache poisoning — spoofed responses redirect users to attacker sites
questions.correct: SNMP information disclosure — MIB data reveals topology, routes, and config details
questions.explanation: SNMP on port 161 can expose detailed network topology, interface stats, ARP tables, and routing data. SNMPv1/v2c use cleartext community strings. SNMPv3 with authPriv should be used and public access blocked.

questions.question: An organization wants to ensure email clients submit mail securely with authentication. Which port should be required?
answers: TCP port 587 — SMTP submission with mandatory authentication and STARTTLS
TCP port 25 — SMTP relay used between mail servers without authentication
TCP port 465 — SMTPS with implicit SSL, deprecated in favor of 587
TCP port 993 — IMAPS for encrypted mailbox access and retrieval
questions.correct: TCP port 587 — SMTP submission with mandatory authentication and STARTTLS
questions.explanation: Port 587 is the current standard for authenticated email submission. It requires client credentials and supports STARTTLS for encryption. Port 25 should be reserved for server-to-server relay only.
